address: Manchester (exact address not captured — property 8–10 weeks from keys at meeting date)
lead_profile_text: First Manchester STL property — purchasing a detached 3-bed house with garden and 2-car parking. Currently owns a buy-to-let portfolio in Belfast (apartments unsuitable for STR due to legal/management constraints). Daughter studying at Manchester University. 8–10 weeks to keys at time of meeting.
emotional_profile: Analytical and financially minded — mortgage-to-income calculation was primary lens throughout. Engaged positively; asked substantive questions rather than objection-raising questions. Experienced landlord from Belfast portfolio — will read agreement carefully.
delay_trigger_phrases: follow-up in ~6 weeks when mortgage/handover dates confirmed
delay_timeline: 8–10 weeks to keys from meeting date (2026-05-20); follow-up call in ~6 weeks once mortgage/handover dates confirmed

top_objections.text: What happens in the worst months — can I cover the mortgage in January and March?
top_objections.zac_response: Seasonal pattern presented: worst Jan–Mar, best Jul–Aug, small December uptick. Year-average positive but worst-case months framed around survivability.

top_objections.text: Will the competition analysis hold up — what if occupancy doesn't hit 60%?
top_objections.zac_response: Competitor analysis: avg 40 reviews, 4.4 rating; Stayful targets 4.6–4.7. 60% occupancy projection and 803 Eastbank Tower case study discussed.

top_objections.text: How does the new tenant rights legislation (May 2026) actually help me vs long-let?
top_objections.zac_response: New tenant rights legislation makes long-let riskier; STL avoids these regulatory risks and provides flexibility.

top_questions.text: My mortgage is about 800 to 850 — does it stack up in the slower months?
top_questions.answer: Net revenue ~£1,800/month after platform fees, management, cleaning. Annual profit ~£7,200 after £800 mortgage + ~£400 bills. Jan–Mar are worst months; worst-case months need survivability framing.

top_questions.text: Can I block the property for personal use when my daughter is at Manchester University?
top_questions.answer: Yes — calendar blocking available for personal use at any time.

what_moved_them: Case study — 803 Eastbank Tower Manchester grossed £56,920 gross in 2025; Prasad noted this directly
Business rates vs council tax guidance included in service
Personal use calendar blocking for when daughter is at university
2–3 week setup turnaround — quicker than expected
offer_framing: No offer made at meeting. Standard rate 15%+VAT, 6-month minimum, 3-month notice, no onboarding fees.
